responses.question: In a typical week, what is the total number of hours you work across all your roles combined?
How many hours per week do you spend face-to-face (or on calls) with customers — across presales and delivery?
What are the main activities that make up your customer-facing time?
responses.answer: Interact with In-Region stakeholders at this point; attend client SteerCo only on the need basis

responses.question: How many hours per week do you typically spend on your Cloud Leader responsibilities?
In your Cloud Leader role, what are the top 3 activities where you spend most of your time and feel you add real value?
responses.answer:
1. Building strategy about the evolution of the practice & ensuring that we marry this to execution
2. Project Health Reviews & interact with In-Region based on the outcomes & in conjunction with DP team
3. Talent Enablement specifically focussing at grooming the next level of leadership, driving the right mindset for E2E ownership; additionally supporting proposals as needed

responses.question: Still in your Cloud Leader role — what are 2 activities you regularly have to do, but feel are low-value or unproductive?
responses.answer:
1. Ensuring we have right resource placements across the opportunities
2. Reviewing resumes for the external hiring

responses.question: How many hours per week do you typically spend on your Delivery Partner responsibilities?
In your Delivery Partner role, what are the top 3 activities where you spend most of your time and feel you add real value?
responses.answer:
1. Driving key strategic relationship with Delivery Leaders in the region
2. QBR / SteerCo ensuring we perform Project Health Reviews & we pre-emptively identify any gaps
3. Identify any opportunities for upselling or exploring the use of GDC accelerators / AI solutions on our engagements

responses.question: Still in your Delivery Partner role — what are 2 activities you regularly have to do, but feel are low-value or unproductive?
responses.answer:
1. Building decks for the reviews
2. Driving the right placements for the resources or identifying replacements in case of any urgencies

responses.question: What is the single biggest thing that prevents you from spending time on what matters most in your role?
responses.answer: Nothing as such other than the operational issues & the time spent in escalation management - it derails the progress & takes us back to focus on fixing the gaps than to really think about the evolution of practice.

responses.question: If you could redesign your week, what would you spend significantly more time on — and what would you hand off or stop doing?
responses.answer:
Continue to plug myself into the strategic initiatives that help strengthen or evolve our practice; gain better understanding about the broader PS initiatives to reflect on how we are stitched into the overall strategy.

Spend more time interacting with the in-region stakeholders & / or customers to represent us & solve the real problems!

Spend more time on my personal enablement as well

Delegate responsibilities w.r.t. resourcing on new opportunities & any other tactical operational activity
